description: Per-site SLA report for customer sharing. Select the site, set the reporting period, then share via Public Dashboard link or export as PDF.

options.content:
**Operator tip — marking a maintenance window:**

Use the Grafana annotations API (most reliable across all versions):

```
curl -X POST https://<grafana-url>/api/annotations \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <api-token>' \
  -d '{
    "text": "Planned maintenance",
    "tags": ["maintenance", "<site-name-or-all>"],
    "time": <epoch_ms_start>,
    "timeEnd": <epoch_ms_end>
  }'
```

Create a Grafana API token at: **⚙ Configuration → API Keys → Add API key** (role: Editor).

Convert times at [epochconverter.com](https://epochconverter.com) — paste the ms value.

Tag with `maintenance` (required — this is what the dashboards filter on) plus the site name or `all` for your own reference. The blue shaded band appears on every monitoring dashboard that has the maintenance annotation layer; the extra tag is organisational only.

Alternatively in Grafana UI: hold **Ctrl** (Windows/Linux) or **⌘ Cmd** (Mac) and drag on a timeseries panel, then add the tags above — but this requires Editor role and may not work in all Grafana versions.

panels.description:
Average uptime percentage over the selected reporting period, calculated using the login-page content probe (Tier 2). This probe checks that the ERPNext login page is fully rendered — not just that the server responds. Both complete outages and degraded states (server responds but application is broken) count against this figure.

Note: if the site was recently added to monitoring, this percentage reflects the monitored window only — partial-month figures are expected for new sites.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description:
Whether the site met the 99.5% monthly uptime SLA target over the selected period.

• COMPLIANT (green) — uptime ≥ 99.5% — at most 3h 36m of downtime in a 30-day month
• BREACH (red) — uptime < 99.5% — downtime exceeded the SLA allowance

Planned maintenance windows marked on the graphs below can be referenced when discussing SLA with customers.
panels.title: SLA Status (99.5% target)

panels.description:
Total cumulative downtime in minutes over the selected reporting period. Calculated as: (1 − uptime fraction) × total seconds in period ÷ 60.

SLA allowance for 99.5% over 30 days = 216 minutes (3h 36m).
Thresholds: green = within SLA, orange = approaching limit, red = SLA breach.
panels.title: Total Downtime (minutes)

panels.description:
Continuous probe result over time: 1 = site UP (login page fully rendered), 0 = site DOWN or DEGRADED (probe failed).

What you will see:
• A flat line at 1.0 — site has been consistently available
• A sharp drop to 0 — an outage or degraded period; the drop is instantaneous (step interpolation) so the exact start/end time is visible
• No line at all — monitoring was not yet configured for this period (common for recently onboarded sites; adjust the time range to the period monitoring was active)

Blue shaded bands = planned maintenance windows (marked by operators). These periods may be excluded from SLA calculations when discussing with customers.

To mark a maintenance window: use the Grafana annotations API with tags ["maintenance", "site-name"] (or "all" for a global window). See the operator tip panel at the top of this dashboard for the full curl command.
panels.title: Availability (login probe success)

panels.description:
Same probe data as the Availability graph above, displayed as colour-coded bands instead of a line.

How to read it:
• Green band = site was UP during that period
• Red band = site was DOWN or DEGRADED during that period
• Consecutive identical states are merged into one block, so a brief outage appears as a thin red sliver
• The width of a red band is proportional to its duration — useful for estimating impact at a glance

Blue shaded bands = planned maintenance windows. These are distinct from red bands and indicate scheduled downtime that may be excluded from SLA discussions.

To mark a maintenance window: use the Grafana annotations API with tags ["maintenance", "site-name"] (or "all" for a global window). See the operator tip panel at the top of this dashboard for the full curl command.
panels.title: Up / Down Timeline

title: GLerp — Customer SLA Report
